title: Diagnosis of abnormal A/C system refrigerant pressure

blocks.text: Diagnosis of abnormal A/C system refrigerant pressure

rows.text: anomalous phenomenon
situation
probable cause
treatment
Abnormally high discharge pressure
High pressure is higher than the ambient air temperature, and when the compressor is stopped, the pressure is 0.19 MPa (1.94 kgf/cm). 2 (about 1.5 mm) drops immediately, and the rest drops slowly.
Air is mixed in the cycle.

Using CFC gas recovery and recycling equipment Recover refrigerant Shih, vacuuming After that, make sure that air does not enter the system again until the specified amount is reached. fill (up) be just about to
Pressure does not normalize when condenser is cooled with water
Overfilling of refrigerant
Using CFC gas recovery and recycling equipment Recover refrigerant and again up to the specified amount. fill (up) be just about to
Radiator Poor fan air suction
Debris is accumulating in the fin section of the capacitor. Radiator Fan rotation failure
Clean the capacitor Check voltage and rotation
Liquid piping is not so hot, but only the condenser area is too hot.
Clogged in the cycle.
Unclogging of all equipment and piping
Low discharge pressure
When the compressor is shut down, the pressure on the high pressure side and the low pressure side quickly become balanced.
Damaged compressor discharge valve and inlet valve Defective compressor seal
Compressor replacement
No frost at the outlet of the expansion valve and negative pressure on the low pressure side
Freezing of expansion valves Failed expansion valve
Expansion valve replacement
Intake pressure is abnormally low
No frost on expansion valves, low-pressure piping is not cold, and low-pressure gauges are vacuumed.
Freezing of expansion valves Failed expansion valve
Expansion valve replacement
Low outlet temperature and no airflow
Evaporator is frozen.
Stop the compressor until defrosting is possible, and rotate only the fan.
Frost on expansion valve
Clogged expansion valve
Clean (remove moisture) or Expansion valve replacement
Cold near the compressor on the low pressure side hose compared to the outlet of the expansion valve and the evaporator
Crumpled or clogged low pressure side hose
Clean or replace low pressure side hose
Intake pressure is too high.
Low pressure hose and check joint area are cooler than evaporator area.
Expansion valve is open too far
Expansion valve replacement
High pressure side pressure is also high, but cooling the condenser (applying water) lowers the inlet pressure.
Overfilling of refrigerant
Using CFC gas recovery and recycling equipment Recover refrigerant and again up to the specified amount. fill (up) be just about to
When the compressor is shut down, the high pressure pressure balances quickly with the low pressure
Damaged gasket High pressure valve damaged Foreign matter caught in the valve on the high pressure side
Compressor replacement
